text: As a process executes, it changes {{c1::state}}
{{c1::Parent}} processes create {{c2::children}} processes, which in turn create other processes, forming a {{c3::tree}} of processes
The {{c1::CPU scheduler}} selects from among the processes in ready queue, and allocates a CPU core to one of them
Preemptive scheduling algorithms can result in {{c1::race}} conditions.

Deadlock can arise if four conditions hold simultaneously.
{{c1::Mutual Exclusion}}: only one process at a time can use a resource.
{{c2::Hold and wait}}: a process holding at least one resource is waiting to acquire additional resources held by other processes
{{c3::No preemption}}: a resource can be released only voluntarily by the process holdign it, after that process has completed its task
{{c4::Circular wait}}: There exists a set {P0, P1, … Pn} of waiting processes such that P0 is waiting for a resource that is held by P1, P1 is waiting for a resource that is held by P2, .., Pn-1 is waiting for a resource that is held by Pn and Pn is waiting for a resource that is held by P0
A program must be brought from {{c1::disk}} into  {{c2::memory}} and placed within a {{c3::process}} for it to be run
